summary: P1 bug fix verified: GET /api/inquiries now returns 200 (not 500) on legacy DB rows where customer_email is empty string, whitespace, or null. Added focused pytest suite (6 tests) at /app/backend/tests/test_inquiries_legacy_email_fix.py covering all requested scenarios. Ran regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_authz_ssrf_ratelimit.py (21/21 PASS). Total: 27/27 passing. Backend-only testing as requested.

bug_fix_verification.POST /api/inquiries with valid EmailStr: 200 OK — created, persisted, visible via GET ✅
bug_fix_verification.POST /api/catalogue-request → row visibl: 200 OK; row stored with customer_email='' is coerced to None on GET; GET does not 500 ✅

critical_code_review_comments: server.py:264 — Inquiry.customer_email: Optional[EmailStr] = None with field_validator _blank_email_to_none (mode='before') correctly coerces '', whitespace, and null to None BEFORE EmailStr validation. Clean fix at the model layer, no changes needed in the GET handler or the /catalogue-request writer.

server.py:284 — InquiryCreate still requires EmailStr for new POST /api/inquiries submissions — intentional and correct; blank emails from /catalogue-request bypass this model by writing directly to the collection.

server.py:560 — /catalogue-request continues to write customer_email='' into the inquiries collection. This is the root cause of the legacy rows; the fix is now on the READ side rather than normalizing at write time. Alternative (not required): normalize the write side to store None instead of '' for future rows — would be more idiomatic but no functional benefit since the field_validator now handles both.
test_credentials: Admin session token 'test_admin_session' inserted directly into MongoDB user_sessions with email raks.agarwal325@gmail.com (matches ADMIN_EMAILS). Used via Cookie: session_token=test_admin_session + X-Requested-With: fetch header.
seed_data_creation: Seeded 3 legacy inquiry rows directly in MongoDB: TEST_legacy_blank_email (customer_email=''), TEST_legacy_null_email (customer_email=null), TEST_legacy_whitespace_email (customer_email='   '). All cleaned up post-test. Also created and cleaned 1 catalogue-request lead and 1 normal inquiry generated by test runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for GET /api/inquiries 500 on legacy blank-email rows is fully verified. New regression file at tests/test_inquiries_legacy_email_fix.py — keep this file for future iterations. Combined with tests/test_authz_ssrf_ratelimit.py (21 tests), total suite is now 27 passing. If you re-seed legacy rows for future testing, prefix ids with TEST_ so cleanup queries (deleteMany on id starting with TEST_ or customer_name starting with TEST_) will remove them.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: /api/catalogue-request writes customer_email='' directly into the inquiries collection (server.py:561). When GET /api/inquiries deserializes the collection back via response_model=List[Inquiry], the old Inquiry model had customer_email: EmailStr which raises pydantic.ValidationError on '' → FastAPI converts to 500. FIX (applied by main agent): Made customer_email Optional[EmailStr]=None + added a field_validator with mode='before' that returns None for empty/whitespace strings, so EmailStr validation is skipped for legacy data. InquiryCreate (POST payload model) still enforces EmailStr — new submissions cannot omit or blank the email. REPRODUCTION: (1) POST /api/catalogue-request with valid name+phone. (2) GET /api/inquiries as admin. Pre-fix: 500. Post-fix: 200 with customer_email=null. VERIFICATION: 6 pytest cases covering all edge cases (blank, whitespace, null, missing, valid, catalogue-request full flow) all pass.
